visual_notes: Hexagonal cross-section is diagnostic in hand specimens. Prismatic crystals can be enormous in pegmatites (multi-kilogram). Emerald is densely included compared to other varieties. Color zoning (core-to-rim or sector) occurs in some crystals.
prompt_fragments: hexagonal beryl prism in forest green
vibe_notes: Beryl is a color-variety powerhouse — a single species that spans the gem rainbow from green to blue to pink to gold, all from trace chromophore swaps in the same hexagonal prism lattice.
folklore_summary: Ancient legend held that wearing an emerald could reveal truth — placing it under the tongue would supposedly make a person speak truthfully. Aquamarine was believed to be the treasure of mermaids.
